steps.title: Extract given information from diagram and problem
steps.content:
From the diagram: AC = 28 (the hypotenuse labeled on the figure) and there is a right angle at B.
From the problem: $AB = AC - 11 = 28 - 11 = 17$. Point $D$ lies on $\overline{AC}$ with $\overline{BD} \perp \overline{AC}$ (neither $D$ nor $\overline{BD}$ is drawn).

steps.title: Identify the similar triangles
steps.content: In right triangle $ABC$, drawing the altitude $\overline{BD}$ from the right angle to the hypotenuse creates triangle $BDC$. Triangle $BDC$ shares angle $C$ with $ABC$ and has a right angle at $D$, so $\triangle ABC \sim \triangle BDC$.

steps.title: Apply the proportionality from similar triangles
steps.content:
Matching corresponding sides (across-from-equal-angles), the ratio of the hypotenuses equals the ratio of the legs opposite angle $C$:
$$\frac{BC}{BD} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{28}{17}$$
The answer is 28/17.
